title: alkanna tinctoria subsp. lehmannii
intro: Alkanna tinctoria subsp. lehmannii, also known as the Alkanna or Ornamental Alkanna, is a subspecies of the Alkanna tinctoria plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the borage family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to be effective in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Alkanna tinctoria subsp. lehmannii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and even have antimicrobial properties. Alkanna tinctoria subsp. lehmannii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
